summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on October 5, 2021, addressing multiple zoning cases including a PBDD final development plan update, a zone change request for self-storage facilities on State Route 28, a major adjustment to the Village of Belmont PUD for new townhome construction by Drees Homes, a minor PUD adjustment for a patio enclosure, and a final development plan review for Barclay Woods. Residents voiced significant concerns about traffic, road access (particularly the opening of State Route 28 and potential closure of Fay Road), HOA integration, and neighborhood safety in the Village of Belmont development.
decisions: ZC 2021-03: No action taken; applicant must restart PBDD and FDP approval process from scratch per prosecutor's guidance
ZC 2021-04: Motion passed to continue case; applicant to pursue PUD zoning instead of M1 for self-storage facility at 7098 State Route 28, with conditions to return next month with modified plans
ZC 2021-05: Motion passed to approve the major adjustment to the Village of Belmont PUD with T intersection at end of Charles Snyder as recommended by county engineer
ZC 2021-06 (Richard Woodward, 3046 Abbey Way): Motion passed to approve minor PUD adjustment for 12x14 patio enclosure with rear yard variance
ZC 249: Final development plan for Cove of Barclay Woods reviewed with conditions that applicant meet with staff to finalize remaining plan details
action_items: Staff (Holbrook) to meet with ZC 2021-03 applicant to go over the PBDD and FDP resubmission process
Eric White (ZC 2021-04) to prepare modified PUD plans with landscaping, parking, retention, and other requirements and resubmit for next month's meeting
ZC 2021-05 recommendation to be forwarded to Board of Trustees with public notification in paper 10 days prior to hearing; adjoining property owners to be notified
Drees Homes (Alex Martin) to coordinate with Choice One engineers on road extension to State Route 28 and CDBG road widening project alignment
Barclay Woods applicant (Choice One) to meet with staff to work through final remaining plan details and add required details to plans
Staff to coordinate with Warren County on ZC 2021-04 self-storage project after township process concludes
key_amounts: $255,000-$300,000: Proposed price range for Drees Homes townhome units in Village of Belmont
$244,000: CDBG grant received by township for road widening projects
1,418 sq ft: End unit size for proposed Drees townhomes
1,750 sq ft: Middle unit size for proposed Drees townhomes
150 feet: Minimum setback required from residential lot lines for self-storage facilities
25-35: Proposed off-street parking spaces for Village of Belmont new section
168 sq ft: Proposed patio enclosure size (12x14) for Woodward variance request
notable_topics: PBDD final development plan status for property at end of Patricia (ZC 2021-03)
Zone change from B1/A1 to PUD for self-storage facility on State Route 28 (ZC 2021-04)
Village of Belmont PUD major adjustment for Phase 2 townhome development by Drees Homes (ZC 2021-05)
Resident concerns about opening State Route 28 access and desire to close Fay Road entrance
Traffic safety concerns including speeding, stop sign compliance, and emergency response times
HOA integration between existing and proposed Village of Belmont sections
Fire chief requirement for two access points per Ohio Fire Code for subdivisions with 30+ dwellings
Minor PUD adjustment for rear patio enclosure variance at 3046 Abbey Way (ZC 2021-06)
Final development plan approval for Cove of Barclay Woods (ZC 249)
Mustang Road modification to cul-de-sac and Charles Snyder T-intersection recommendation
attendees: Deputy Chief Edward Meyer (referenced in staff comments)
Chief Pegram (Fire Chief, referenced in staff comments)
notes: Strong resident opposition to keeping Fay Road open as a second access point to Village of Belmont, with informal show of hands indicating nearly all residents present wanted Fay Road closed if Route 28 access opened. Fire Chief Pegram's letter requires two access points per Ohio Fire Code for subdivisions with 30+ dwellings, creating tension with resident preferences. The meeting applied 2008 zoning resolution standards to PUD cases as required when reviewing previously approved PUDs. Clermont County Planning Commission unanimously recommended approval of the self-storage zone change. Transcript references 'President Biden' at the opening which appears to be a transcription error. The Village of Belmont HOA annual meeting was scheduled for the following day (October 6). The golf course adjacent to Village of Belmont was noted as potential future development but nothing is currently proposed.
title: ZC 10.5.21.mp4
